changes_summary: FLAG-001 through FLAG-003 indicate the original plan was aimed at the wrong root cause in one area: the UI click-to-select affordance already exists, so adding a new `TimelineCanvas` callback would duplicate working code instead of fixing the missing shot-context pipeline. The revised plan removes that stale frontend scope, treats shot names as external data that must be loaded explicitly on the backend, and centralizes same-shot resolution so `runAgentLoop`, `create_task`, `set_lora`, and `duplicate_generation` all use the same shot-context rules rather than patching only `duplicate_generation`.

success_criteria.criterion: `describeTimeline()` output includes a `Shot groups:` section listing each pinned shot group with resolved `shotName` (or `shotId` fallback), `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode` when `pinnedShotGroups` exist.
Each clip line in the timeline summary includes shot metadata when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group.
`SelectedClipPayload` and persisted `AgentTurn.attachments` accept optional shot id/name fields without breaking existing consumers.
The frontend selection send path (`useSelectedMediaClips` -> `AgentChat` -> `useSendMessage`) preserves `shot_id` and `shot_name` for timeline-selected clips.
Backend selected-clip normalization and continuation recovery preserve shot metadata across turns.
`buildSelectedClipsPrompt()` includes shot info when `shot_id` or `shot_name` is available.

Same-shot resolution is centralized and used by `runAgentLoop`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`, preferring explicit shot metadata and falling back to generation lookup only when needed.
`executeDuplicateGeneration()` skips `findShotForGenerations()` when selected clips already provide a shared `shot_id`.
Clicking a shot-group label continues to select all clips in that group.
All existing tests in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/` continue to pass.

New or updated unit tests cover timeline shot-group summaries, selected-clip shot metadata roundtrip, selected-clip prompt shot context, duplicate-generation's direct `shot_id` path, and the frontend send path.
The system prompt mentions shared-shot context guidance once structured shot metadata is available.
No new files exceed roughly 300 lines.
Manual smoke test: select a shot group, send an agent message, and verify shot context survives into a continuation turn.

assumptions: Existing single-click shot-group selection semantics are correct and should be preserved rather than redesigned in this revision.
Backend code can batch-load shot names from the `shots` table by `shotId` without storing shot names inside `TimelineConfig`.
Timeline-selected clips can resolve shot membership from `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups`, while gallery-only selections may still require generation-based fallback.
Explicit selected-clip `shot_id` should take precedence over generation-based inference whenever both are available.
Vitest remains the test harness for both frontend and edge-function coverage.
